# Dashboard exploratory analysis

Deterministic exploratory analysis derived from `Data/MIS_Ideology_2026-04-08.csv` and `Data/MIS_Ideology_party_data_2026-04-08.csv`.

## Key findings

- Ideological mean is not a monotonic story: it peaks in 1996 (0.3089), bottoms in 2010 (0.0556), and closes 2024 at 0.1823.
- Ideological dispersion (`ideo_imp_sd`) and polarization measures are related but not identical. Dispersion is highest in 1998 (0.2248) and rises again by 2024 (0.2087).
- Polarization metrics in the raw municipal file (`pol_dalton_norm`, `pol_esteban_ray`, `pol_range`) have different temporal peaks than simple ideology dispersion, so the dashboard should not collapse them into one line.
- Fragmentation is a strong late-cycle story: municipal ENEP peaks in 2016 (7.6308), while party-based national NEP also peaks in 2016 (21.0344) and remains far above 1994 levels by 2024 (15.0890).
- The ideological center expands around 2008-2010 and contracts sharply by 2016, then partially recovers by 2024.
